status: P1 EVIDENCE CLOSURE WAVE 7 / SEQUENCE OR SUPPLIER-EQUIVALENCE BLOCK ACTIVE / NOT PLATINUM CERTIFIED
classification: Supplier-named neural / cortical short regulatory peptide family
mechanism_summary: No canonical mechanism is assigned. The product name suggests an organ-targeted commercial positioning, but exact sequence, target and pharmacology require direct primary-source and analytical closure.
evidence_maturity: Regional short-peptide, cell, animal or limited human literature may exist under this name or related names. Evidence is not attributed to the catalogue product until chemical identity and source linkage are confirmed.
regulatory_headline: IDENTITY-SENSITIVE SUPPLIER PRODUCT FAMILY / NO UNIVERSAL APPROVAL OR ORGAN-TREATMENT CLAIM
certification_blockers: Molecular formula, mass and reference standard
Current product- and jurisdiction-specific regulatory status
closure_class: AEDP TETRAPEPTIDE / CORTICAL POSITIONING AND EVIDENCE QUALITY
closure_state: PARTIAL CLOSURE — CORTAGEN LITERATURE IDENTITY ALA-GLU-ASP-PRO (AEDP) AND CELL/ANIMAL PROGRAMME IDENTIFIED; COR20 TERMINI, SALT, ANALYTICAL IDENTITY, STERILITY AND CONTROLLED HUMAN EVIDENCE OPEN
authoritative_product_boundary: Primary publications identify Cortagen as Ala-Glu-Asp-Pro (AEDP) and describe cell, animal and transcriptomic experiments. One publication abstract refers to human post-traumatic nerve recovery, but the available record does not close trial design, registration, exact product identity, independent replication or COR20 equivalence.
critical_closure_controls: AEDP literature identity is not COR20 analytical proof
Animal, cell and transcriptomic findings are not controlled human efficacy
No nerve-repair, cognition, stroke or neuroprotection claim
No dosing, injection or reconstitution protocol
